Evan M. Wooten, Esq. of Mayer Brown LLP, Tel. 213.621.9450, for defendant, via Court Call.

RULING: At the hearing, Plaintiff's counsel exercised a 170.6 challenge to Judge Robertson. The clerk was ordered by the Court to file plaintiff's motion for peremptory challenge per CCP 170.6 in open court. Judge Robertson accepted the challenge, vacated the tentative ruling, and instructed the parties to agree on a future date. The matter was recalled and per the parties' agreement, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SAUNDERS INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF A CLASS AND SUBCLASS OF SIMILARLY SITUATED INDIVIDUALS' Re-Noticed Motion For Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs, Class Representatives' Enhancement And Claims Administration Fees was continued to August 25, 2014 at 9:30 am in Department 302 to be heard by Judge Goldsmith. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STUBHUB, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Eric A. Grover of Keller Grover LLP (543.1305) for plaintiff and responding party; Evan M. Wooten and John Nadolenco of Mayer Brown (213.229.9500), and Brandon Pace of eBay, Inc. (801.545.1658) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained as to the first cause of action and overruled as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff concedes that, at least at this point, his first cause of action lacks merit. Defendant's interpretation of Penal Code 632.7(b)(2) is contrary to the plain language of that statute and would required the court to ignore that language. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
